ADJ.etymology: From stor, from stórr, from *stōraz, from *stāro-.
Since at least 1621, from braf; from brav; from brave, borrowed from bravo.
From viss, from *gawissaz. Cognate with viss.
From svār, svāger, svor, from svárr, from *swēraz, from *swer-. Compare schwer.
From verankern or verankeren, equivalent to ankra. Cognate with forankre, forankre and forankra, forankre.
From næstr, a superlative of ná, corresponding to *nēhwist.
From tíðligr and tidelik, related to tid.
From dáligr, compare with dårlig, dårlig, dåleg.
From flere, flæster, from fleiri, from *flaizô, derived from *pleh₁-. Cognate, among others, with fleiri, flestr, plūs and πλέως, πλεῖστος.
From belasten. Equivalent to lasta. Cognate with belaste and belasten.
From eiginn, from *aiganaz, past participle of *aiganą. Equivalent to -en. Cognate with own.

From enkil, from enkel, from *ainakulaz, equivalent to en + -el. Compare etymology of simple. Cognate with enkel, enkelt, enkel, ekkil, enkel and 𐌰𐌹𐌽𐌰𐌺𐌻𐍃. Compare also singulus with the same suffix. See also änka and änk

hemsker, derived from hem. The original meaning was equivalent to homely, ergo "one who has only stayed at home" or "one who has not been out in the world", and subsequently "stupid" or "easily terrified". Cognate of hei
From hǿgri, comparative of hǿgr, from *hōgiz. Cognate with hægri, højre and høyre.
Contraction of litet, neuter of liten. Compare mycket, with similar formation.
From mena, from mēnen, meinen (compare meina, mene), from mēnian, from *mainijaną, from *mein-.

Attested since 1762. Derived from ''mysa'' ("to smile (slightly) with contentedness/comfort").''Svenska Akademiens ordbok'', column [https://web.archive.org/web/20160305023008/http://g3.spraakdata.gu.se/saob/show.phtml?f
From mærkeliker, from merkiligr. Compare merkeleg, mærkelig and merkelig.
-lig. Note that the verb rimma is not only used in the literal sense "to rhyme" but also in the figurative sense "to fit".
Borrowed from the stem of snaben.807
From the past participle of ställa.
Originally the past participle form of särskilja.
From þrir, þrī, from Runic Swedish þrīR, from þrír, from ᚦᚱᛁᛃᛟᛉ, from *þrīz, ultimately from *tréyes. Compare Norwegian and Danish tre, Icelandic þrír, Faroese tríggir.
weldich, whence also vældig and veldig (which are usually used in a different way from the Swedish word). See the related gewaltig for more.
From ǣrlīker, from êrlich, from *aiʀulīk, equivalent to -lig. Cognate of ehrlich.
From ø̄dhkænder‚ from auðkendr, from kenna. Cognate with åkjendt, audkjend and auðkenndur.
überraschen, equivalent to raska. Cognate of overraske.

ADP.etymology: From som or sum, in Runic inscriptions also ''sim'', same as sem, from sem. Also related to the prefix sam- and suffix -sam. Still in the Poetic Edda, the Icelandic ''sem'' is only used as a comparative particle, e.g. Há
From i mot, equivalent to mot.

ADV.etymology: From iū, io. Compare jo, jo, ja (adverb) / je (conjunction).
Corresponding to och / ock + så, from ok svā, from ok and svá. Compare også.
Shortened form of huru, from hwaru, from neuter dative of *hwar
Contraction of litet, neuter of liten. Compare mycket, with similar formation.
From vel, from *wela, from *welh₁-. Common Germanic word. Cognate with vel, wel, well, væl, wohl, 𐍅𐌰𐌹𐌻𐌰, vel, vel, vel.
weldich, whence also vældig and veldig (which are usually used in a different way from the Swedish word). See the related gewaltig for more.
From bara, from *bara, from *barr, from *bazaz. Compare bara.
From gans, gansch, from ganz, from ganz, from *gant.
From då. Compare endå, enda, endda, enda.
Since at least 1621, from braf; from brav; from brave, borrowed from bravo.
From nær, from *nēhwiz, comparative of *nēhw. Ultimately from *h₂neḱ-.
Borrowed from also. Compare allt and så. Cognate to altså and altso.
From þær for, equivalent to för. Compare therefore, derfor, dafür.
The adverb has developed from a verb usage meaning "it is understood" (describing a statement).
From nóg, nógr, gnógr, from *ganōgaz. Ultimately from *h₂eh₂nóḱe, perfective of *h₂neḱ-.

From til baka, from til + baka, genitive plural of baker, equivalent to baks, according to SAOB online the alternative forms with -s are however not hence this fact, but rather from the genitive singular. Cognate with ti
From even, effen, from evan, from *ebn, from *ebnaz, cognate with even, even. Compare effen. See also jämn.
From aldra, the genitive plural of all, from allr. Originally "of all" or "among all".
From alz, influenced by Low German alles.
pretty From grannr, with semantic shift "thin, fine" > "fine, pretty".
gång, also written as separate words

From som or sum, in Runic inscriptions also ''sim'', same as sem, from sem. Also related to the prefix sam- and suffix -sam. Still in the Poetic Edda, the Icelandic ''sem'' is only used as a comparative particle, e.g. Há
From til saman, til samans, from tosamene. Cognate with tilsammen, til sammen, tilsammen, zusammen, tousommen, tosamen, tezamen and zesummen.
From at minsto, + the feminine dative singular definite suffix definite ''-unni/-onne''. Equivalent to åt + dative form of modern minsta.
From aldri, aldrigi. Cognate with aldrei, aldri, aldri and aldrig.
From tid, literally "all time". Compare altid, altijd, ætíð, alltid, alltid.
From annorlund, annerlunda, anner lundh or lunda
From þit, from þít, according to SAOB likely from þí + at. þí is in turn an old locative, possibly related to 𐌸𐌴𐌹, and more distantly to τεῖ in τεῖδε. Equivalent to ty + åt.
From drygher, from drjúgr, from *dreugaz. Cognate to друг.
From i mot, equivalent to mot.
From gjarna, from *gernô, the adverbial form of *gernaz.
From í gær, í gjár (whence í gær and í gjár). Akin to går.
A contraction of earlier neder, from niðr, from *niþer, from *niter.
From sinn. Note that ''sinn'' is a neuter noun, and ''någon'' is thus the old neuter plural form. Compare någonting.
From not, nut, from *hnōjaną, from *kneh₂-. Cognate with not, not, not, nót and Nut.not
From richtich or richtig. Compare rigtig.
From tíðligr and tidelik, related to tid.
From ower ens, over en, equivalent of ens.

AUX.etymology: From koma, from koma, from *kwemaną, from *gʷem-. orgasm is probably a come.
bliva, from bliva, from blîven, from bilīvan, from *bilīban, from *bilībaną. varda completely in the present tense, and partly in the past tense.

CCONJ.etymology: From som or sum, in Runic inscriptions also ''sim'', same as sem, from sem. Also related to the prefix sam- and suffix -sam. Still in the Poetic Edda, the Icelandic ''sem'' is only used as a comparative particle, e.g. Há

DET.etymology: From not, nut, from *hnōjaną, from *kneh₂-. Cognate with not, not, not, nót and Nut.not

NOUN.etymology: From vrāge (Frage), from *frāgu, from *frēgō. Compare frain. Displaced native spörja, spörsmål.
From del, from dēl, deil, from dēl.
krîch. Unlike and krig, the Swedish word has neutrum gender like kríggj.
Borrowed from critique, from criticus, from κριτικός.
Related to ligga and lägga, like German Lage.
sǫk, from *sakō. Cognate with sak, sak, sag, sök, sake, zaak, Saak, Sook, Sache. An unrelated word that also underwent the transformation in meaning from "legal matter" to "thing, item" is causa.
From stellen, from stellian. Cf Stelle.
From Bild, from bilde, from biladi, bilidi. beläte.
From raust, ultimately from a - derivative of *h₃reyH-.922 Related to *runsiz. Cognate with røst.
From spil, from spil and spil, from *spil.
From alvara, from an adjective ǫlværr, maybe from alwār.
From boðskapr. Akin to skap, like Botschaft.
From krapter, from kraptr, krǫptr, from *kraftuz.
From quæld, qvælder, from kveld, from *kwildiz, from *gʷelH-.
From not, nut, from *hnōjaną, from *kneh₂-. Cognate with not, not, not, nót and Nut.not
From skæmt, from skæmta, whence also modern skämta. Cognate with skæmt, skjemt and skjemt.
From sats. Cognate with slutsats, sluttsats, sluttsats, Schlusssatz, Schluss-Satz.
From time, from tími, from *tīmô, from *tī-, from *dī-.

PRON.etymology: From som or sum, in Runic inscriptions also ''sim'', same as sem, from sem. Also related to the prefix sam- and suffix -sam. Still in the Poetic Edda, the Icelandic ''sem'' is only used as a comparative particle, e.g. Há

SCONJ.etymology: From som or sum, in Runic inscriptions also ''sim'', same as sem, from sem. Also related to the prefix sam- and suffix -sam. Still in the Poetic Edda, the Icelandic ''sem'' is only used as a comparative particle, e.g. Há
From nær, from *nēhwiz, comparative of *nēhw. Ultimately from *h₂neḱ-.

VERB.etymology: From finnas, from finnask. Cognate with finnes and finnast.
From praten, probably of sv origin. Compare prate and prattle, práta.
bliva, from bliva, from blîven, from bilīvan, from *bilīban, from *bilībaną. varda completely in the present tense, and partly in the past tense.
From þykkia, þykia, from þykkja, from *þunkijaną.
From gøra, gæra, giora, from gera, gøra, gørva, from *garwijaną. Compare with yare and gear. * The noun is a nominalization of the verb. Literally "doing."
From tro, from trú, from *trūwō.
From värkia, from verka, from *werką, from *wérǵom. Compare werken and wirken.
From hænda, from henda, from *handijaną.
From koma, from koma, from *kwemaną, from *gʷem-. orgasm is probably a come.
Borrowed from anwenden. Compare anvende.
From fār, from fár, from *fawaz.
From byria, from byrja, from *burjaną. Derived from *bʰer-.
From halda, halla, holda, holla, from halda, from *haldaną. Further etymology uncertain; possibly from *ḱel-.
From spila, borrowed from spillen, from spilōn, from *spilōn. Equivalent to a.
From vita, from vita, from *witaną, ultimately from *weyd-.
From gilda, from gilda. Compare gilda and gildr.
sätta
From föra, probably after durchführen, whence also doorvoeren.
From handla, from handla, hǫndla, from *handlōną. In the sense ''trade'' influenced by from handelen and handeln.
a
From køra, from keyra, from *kaurijaną, *karjaną. More at cair.
sv
Compare pege and peika.
From vælia, from velja, from *waljaną. Causative of *wiljaną (see vilja).
era
Through apocope with umlaut from forstanda, from vörstenden, from farstandan. Cognate with forstå. Equivalent to stå.
söka or a
verändern, equivalent to ändra. Compare forandre.
From heta, from heita, from *haitaną.
From hiælpa, from hjalpa, from *helpaną, from *ḱelb-, *ḱelp-.
From hōpen, from *hopon, from *hopōn.
Verbalization of jobb. Compare jobbe and jobba/jobbe.
Shortened from kollationera.
Passive of känna. Literally, "to be felt."
From lova, from lofa, from *lubōną, equivalent to -a. Cognate with love, love, loven, loben.
a
From skriva, from skrifa, from *skrībaną, a late borrowing from scrībō. Cognate with skrive, skriva, skrive, skrifa, skriuwe, shrive, schrieven, schrijven and schreiben.
Borrowed from stemmen, from *stemnian, from *stabnijan, from *stabnijaną.
fylla
From ändern. Cognate with ændre.
era
From bevinden, from bifindan. Equivalent to finna. Cognate with befinne, befinde, befinden, bevinden, befind and befiende.

From berætta, from berichten, in correspondence to rätta (verb) and rätt (verb and noun), equivalent to -a or rätta. Cognate with berette, berette, berichten, berechten, bericht, berig, berichten, Bericht and beriichten.
stämma, like German bestimmen
tona
From bruken, from brukan.
From dragha, from draga, from *draganą, from *dʰregʰ-. In some senses, from dragen (compare tragen).
From flytia, from flytja, from *flutjaną.
From vrāge (Frage), from *frāgu, from *frēgō. Compare frain. Displaced native spörja, spörsmål.

From the ''funk-'' in funktion which is borrowed from function, from fonction, from functiōnem, accusative of functiō, from functus perfect participle of fungor. fungera, which is borrowed from fungor via fungieren.
handla
From hitta, from hitta, from *hittijaną, from *keyd-.
From iæmføra.
sv.
lucke, gelucke. According to the OED, it may be related to the source of lock (compare *lūkaną). Compare and lykke, and lukke. Compare also luck, Glück, גליק, gelok, and geluk, and koloku.
From mena, from mēnen, meinen (compare meina, mene), from mēnian, from *mainijaną, from *mein-.
sätta
peka
verka
agera
From raþa, from ráða, from *rēdaną.
From røra, from hrœra, from *hrōzijaną.
From ske, from schên, from skehan, from *skehan. Cognate with ske, skje, geschehen, geschieden, sji-jje.
From skikka, from schicken "to send" (schicken, schikken). Cognate with ske "to happen, to occur". Compare Trøndelag sjekk.
From sleppa (past tense sleppti), from *slampijaną, from *(s)lembʰ-, see also *limpaną.
Passive form of slå.
From stoppa, from stoppen, from *stoppon, from *stoppōn.
From styra, from stýra, from *stiurijaną.
From sætia, from setja, from *satjaną. Cognate with set.
From tala, from *talō, from *del-.
höra, like gehören, tilheyra, tilhøre.
Probably sv child-speak, similar to titte.
Borrowed from treffen. dräpa.
